opening_angle: Connect role at Apex Systems to Conner’s strength in building and operating production-grade security tooling that turns ambiguity into reliable remediation workflows, especially where cross-team coordination and automation are needed
paragraph_focus: Direct alignment with security engineer role through operational tool-building and incident response automation
Concrete examples from UCOP: containerized services, AI-assisted tooling with auditability, standardized runbooks for remediation
Earlier automation work and phishing detection project showing pattern of pragmatic engineering with measurable outcomes
Reiteration of core operating principle: ship, document, build guardrails — and how that serves Apex’s needs across cloud, SIEM, API security, and third-party risk
voice_controls: Use active verbs like build, ship, integrate, reconcile, automate — avoid 'passionate about' or vague claims
Replace leadership language with coordination and handoff language (e.g., 'coordinate across teams' not 'lead initiatives')
Anchor every claim in deployable artifacts: runbooks, APIs, pipelines, containerized services
claims_to_avoid: Certification claims (OSCP/GPEN) since evidence shows hands-on but no certification
Abstract statements like 'security-first mindset' without technical grounding
Overstated impact metrics (e.g., 'reduced incidents by X%') without supporting data
Buzzword-heavy AI claims — keep focus on auditability, safety, and operational fit
